title: Intense Dense Thai Chicken Tom Kha
description: A sour and dense Thai dish featuring chicken, flour, and a blend of spices, cooked to perfection using sous-vide and robata-grill methods, this flexitarian-friendly recipe is a twist on traditional tom kha, with the addition of flour to enhance the texture and flavor.
instructions: Preheat the sous-vide machine to 140 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the chicken pieces with salt and coat with flour, shaking off excess.
Seal the chicken in a sous-vide bag with lemon grass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves, and cook for 2 hours.
Grill the robata-grill to high heat and char the chicken pieces for 2 minutes on each side.
In a blender, combine coconut milk, tom kha paste, fish sauce, and lime juice, and blend until smooth.
Add the cooked chicken to the blender and blend until the chicken is coated with the sauce.
Transfer the sauce to a saucepan and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Stir in chopped Thai basil and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the chicken and sauce hot, garnished with additional basil and lemon wedges if desired.
